title: Hydra Dissociation/Reaggregation
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"6ddnu","text":"Note: This protocol was adapted in the Juliano laboratory from published protocols and can tolerate a lot of variation.  ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fii6l","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"c698j","text":"[ST1] This step removes the tentacles, which will not particpate in development of an aggregate.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"rc6s","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6cn9b","text":"[ST2] Aim for reducing the Hydra pellet volume by ~1/2 (i.e. shearing approximately half of the available tissue.) Over dissociation can damage the cells, resulting in a reduction in aggregate survival/development. ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9k0tf","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"eo4h2","text":"[ST3] Depending on the cell density, 50-200 uL aliquots are generally sufficient in size to develop into one or more Hydra. Aggregate size will dictate the number of heads formed. Larger aggregates are more likely to survive, up to a point. Ultimately, you have to play with this step to determine what works best for you and your experiment.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":117,"length":6}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4slu5","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ejimf","text":"[ST4] We have had success with reduced times and speeds.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dgviq","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"asr5a","text":"[ST5] Can alternatively gently pipette to dissociate pellet from the tube wall, but the passive method minimizes further disruptions to the pellet.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"51c1q","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"c9aag","text":"[ST6] The 50/50 mix of DM/HM and 100% DM conditions resulted in similar rates of survival, but it seemed that the 50/50 mix may slightly increase the rate of development.  Immediate transfer to 100% HM resuls in low survival rates.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"btriq","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"a2b10","text":"[ST7] In our hands, aggregates are healthier if provided with antibiotics.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"e30ej","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7sp58","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fmjt4","text":"        NOTE: We used HEPES instead of TES","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":8,"length":34}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"713ip","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ao1c2","text":"· Glass Pasteur pipette","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7jegg","text":"· Eppendorf tubes","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7hc1l","text":"· 24-well plate","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Wash 30 Hydra several times in fresh Hydra Medium (HM) to remove debris
Transfer Hydra to 1.5mL Eppendorf, remove media, and add 1mL 4°C Dissociation Medium (DM)
Incubate Hydra at 4°C for 30-60 min (can incubate several hours without obvious detriment/benefit)
Shear by glass Pasteur pipette ~10-15x until supernatant becomes slightly turbid, allow Hydra to settle, and discard supernatant [ST1- refer to guidelines]

Add 1mL fresh 4°C DM and shear by pipetting up and down with glass pasteur pipette ~1-1.5 min until solution becomes turbid, and approximately half of the available tissue is dissociated [ST2 - refer to guidelines]
Let remaining large pieces settle. The supernatant contains the cells that will be collected and used for reaggregation
Aliquot supernatant (dissociated cell suspension from step 7) to new Eppendorf tubes at an approximate desired cell density [ST3 -refer to guidelines]
Centrifuge aliquots in microcentrifuge at 800g for 6 min [ST4 -refer to guidelines]
Invert tubes for ~5-10 min to allow pellet to passively separate from the tube wall [ST5 -refer to guidelines]
Transfer pellet to a 24-well plate containing a 50/50 mixture of DM and HM+Penicillin/Streptomycin (P/S) or 100% DM [ST6 -refer to guidelines]
Periodically during development, replace the medium with HM+P/S

If starting from 50/50 DM/HM+P/S, depending on the duration of the experiment, make the first medium exchange by 12hpa or 24hpa, and make subsequent exchanges at ~24h intervals for the duration of the experiment.

If starting with 100% DM, exchange into 50/50 DM/HM+P/S at ~4 hours post aggregation (hpa), exhange into HM+P/S 100% at ~16hpa, and then continue to exange medium every ~24 hours [ST7 - refer to guidelines]
